items.title: Health Canada recalls climbing harnesses due to 'fall hazard' that could cause death
items.summary_auto: Health Canada has recalled several types of climbing harnesses, including some for children, due to faulty buckles and attachments causing fall hazards.

items.title: Artemis II crew gets hero's welcome at Canadian Space Agency in Quebec
items.summary_auto: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen and the rest of the Artemis II crew have received a hero's welcome at Canadian Space Agency headquarters after their journey to the far side of the moon.

items.title: 26 more Canadians being contacted for 'low-risk' hantavirus exposure
items.summary_auto: More Canadians are being contacted after having been on the same flight as a confirmed hantavirus case, according to federal health officials.

items.title: More than half of comments removed from Alto's high-speed rail map to be reinstated
items.summary_auto: More than half of the 118 comments removed by moderators from a map showing possible routes for Canada's proposed high-speed rail project should never have been taken down and will be added back by the end of the week, according to Alto.

items.title: Canadian Pacific cleared of liability in Lac-Mégantic train disaster
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Pacific Railway Company has been cleared of all liability in the Lac-Mégantic, Que., train disaster that claimed 47 lives in 2013. Several parties, including the victims and their families, were attempting to sue the rail carrier for its role in the disaster.

items.title: Top doctor asked how public health teams handle 'low-risk' hantavirus exposures, testing
items.summary_auto: Dr. Joss Reimer, Canada's chief public health officer, says her team is working with provincial and territorial counterparts to create national guidance as the country sees both low-risk and higher-risk exposures to hantavirus related to a cluster of cases linked to a cruise ship.

items.title: April home sales down amid frosty start to spring real estate market
items.summary_auto: Home sales were down four per cent last month compared to April 2025, according to the Canadian Real Estate Association. The national average sale price of a home sold in April was $695,412, up 2.2 per cent from last year.

items.title: Shakira, Madonna and BTS to headline 1st Super Bowl-style halftime show at World Cup final
items.summary_auto: Ever heard of a halftime show… for soccer? Turns out FIFA is aiming to make it a thing at The World Cup final this summer. Shakira, Madonna and K-pop superstar-boy band BTS will headline the first World Cup final halftime show at the Metlife Stadium in New Jersey on July 19, FIFA announced Thursday in an Instagram post.

items.title: Here's why you don’t need to worry about a 'super' El Niño — yet
Manitoba teen accused of planning school attack now charged with conspiracy to commit murder
items.summary_auto: A 14-year-old from Rivers, Man., now faces charges of conspiracy to commit murder and counselling the offence of murder. He was initially arrested in March after Interpol and the FBI alerted RCMP to online messages about school attacks in Manitoba and Nova Scotia.

items.title: CBC called out for role in prank interviews of Kamloops residential school grave critics
items.summary_auto: The CBC was blasted on Tuesday for its role in a comedy series that uses fake names and identities to prank individuals who have drawn controversy over their views of residential schools.

items.title: 'Changes are needed': Edmonton Oilers fire head coach Kris Knoblauch
items.summary_auto: The Edmonton Oilers have fired head coach Kris Knoblauch along with assistant coach Mark Stuart.

items.title: Natural gas to play key role in strategy to double Canada’s electricity grid by 2050
Alphonso Davies's return to Canadian lineup complicated by club team Bayern Munich's reported resistance
items.summary_auto: The countdown to Canada’s World Cup opener on June 12 in Toronto is picking up speed, now only weeks rather than months away. Alphonso Davies, sidelined for the third time since February with a muscle tear, will be wishing for time to stop.

items.title: Deer Lake, N.L., bracing for flooding, as mayor says water levels highest in 20 years
items.summary_auto: “We are not in a good position,” said Mayor Dean Ball, about the situation that will see Deer Lake Power open its spill gates over the upcoming long weekend into the Grand Lake Reservoir, which is already seeing high water levels due to melting snow and rain.

items.title: Honda indefinitely suspends $15B EV plant in Ontario
items.summary_auto: Honda has indefinitely suspended its plans for a $15-billion electric vehicle complex in Ontario, as it pivots away from EVs and toward hybrids. The move comes as the automaker posted its first full-year loss ever to the tune of $3.68 billion, in large part due to lower EV demand.

items.title: Zelenskyy's former top aide arrested as Ukraine graft probe widens
items.summary_auto: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's former right-hand man was taken into custody on Thursday on money-laundering charges, part of a fight against graft that is fueling wartime political tensions.

items.title: Biocontrol facility takes aim at invasive insect threatening forest
items.summary_auto: The hemlock woolly adelgid — named because it is covered in white woolly material — can cause foliage loss and even kill eastern hemlock trees. A team at Nova Scotia's Acadia University are rearing specialized predatory insects they're hoping can control these pests. The CBC's Gareth Hampshire suited up to get into the facility.

items.title: Here’s where home invasions are happening in Toronto and what’s being done to prevent them
items.summary_auto: The rise in home invasions in Toronto over the last few years is largely due to a significant increase in home invasions involving houses in some of the city's wealthiest neighbourhoods, according to a CBC News analysis of Toronto police data. Residents of those neighbourhoods have been taking matters into their own hands.

items.title: PWHL introduces new Hamilton franchise, fans thrilled to cheer for new pro hockey team
items.summary_auto: The PWHL announced an expansion to Hamilton Wednesday, alongside an expansion to Las Vegas. Local fans and players say it will be great to cheer for a home team.

items.title: These refugees in Kenya were promised jobs in Canada. At the 11th hour, the employer cancelled the offer
items.summary_auto: A continuing care organization in Nova Scotia has rescinded job offers it had been extending to refugees for more than two years, leaving the displaced people without a path out of some of the largest refugee camps in the world. They were invited to come to Canada through a federal program, but the organization says as processing timelines and the number of people needing care increased, many of the roles were filled locally.

items.title: Cricket Canada's funding frozen by governing body following fifth estate investigation
items.summary_auto: The International Cricket Council issued notice to Cricket Canada that its funding was frozen effective immediately due to concerns over its governance system and a lack of financial oversight.

items.title: Here's where home invasions are happening in Toronto and what's being done to prevent them
items.summary_auto: The rise in home invasions in Toronto over the last few years is largely due to a significant increase in home invasions involving houses in some of the city's wealthiest neighbourhoods, according to a CBC News analysis of Toronto police data. Residents of those neighbourhoods have been taking matters into their own hands.

items.title: Canada wants sovereign AI data centres. What does that actually mean?
items.summary_auto: Canada wants to build data centres that are not just physically located here, but controlled here — a distinction experts say could determine whether the country can reduce its dependence on U.S. tech giants and keep Canadian data subject to Canadian rules.

items.title: Federal Court grants request to group constitutional challenges against new asylum law
items.summary_auto: Immigration lawyers across Canada are co-ordinating a constitutional challenge against Ottawa's new asylum law, with three dozen cases already filed in Federal Court.

items.title: Travel to the U.S. is down even more dramatically than we thought, data shows
items.summary_auto: The boycott by Canadians of travel to the U.S., driven largely by an aversion to President Donald Trump, his industry-wrecking tariffs and 51st state taunts, has actually been more effective than previously thought, according to new data.

items.title: As Canada plans to send election observers to Armenia, advocates warn of a democratic backslide
items.summary_auto: As the federal government prepares to send seven election observers to Armenia, an advocacy group featuring the former president of Human Rights Watch is warning Canada and its Western allies of democratic backsliding prior to the June vote.

items.title: Sobeys, Loblaw under fire for maple washing, as Sobeys ditches maple leaf symbol in stores
items.summary_auto: More than a year after the Buy Canadian movement took root, grocery giants Loblaw and Sobeys are facing increased scrutiny over "maple washing" — the practice of promoting imported goods as homegrown.

items.title: Ontario starts crackdown on ticket resale above face value
items.summary_auto: Letters notifying resellers of inspection were sent out last week, and investigators are starting to inspect ticket resellers and big companies like StubHub this week, according to Minister of Public and Business Service Delivery and Procurement Stephen Crawford. But resale sites say they're still looking for guidance on how to implement the rules.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | See inside the core of the CN Tower and other off-limit areas
items.summary_auto: The CN Tower is giving rare access to areas of the engineering marvel that are normally closed to the public. CBC News photographer Evan Mitsui checked out the tower's lesser known parts during a media preview on Wednesday.
